name: white-up-labelled-corpus
description: Local-path corpus manifest for reproducible recognizer probes. The JPEGs are intentionally not committed yet; expected SHA256 fields catch input drift across runners.

primary.label: native-arm64-macos-python312
primary.notes: Expected scores/categories in this manifest are pinned to native ARM64 macOS with Python 3.12.13, NumPy 2.3.5, and Pillow 12.2.0. Other architectures, including x86_64/Rosetta and Linux serverless targets, can produce architecture-dependent recognition outcomes; see Issue #25.

Canonical-WCA ground truth captured post-PR-#95 (cube-snap); no _canonical_ground_truth_state rotation needed at parse time. Yaw=3. Repair stays score-positive but is manual-review because pre-repair piece evidence is too conflicted for a trusted high-confidence result. captureFrameState preserved in the saved file's recognitionSignals for the photo-frame projection.
Added and calibrated 2026-05-26 from canonical /Users/jhuber/cube-corpus GT expansion. Category/score pinned from tools/probe_corpus.py on current recognizer: success_clean, score 54/54.

Known orientation-rank case; this local hashed pair currently scores above the cross-runner floor, but the repaired candidate is manual-review because pre-repair piece evidence is too conflicted for a trusted high-confidence result.

Second yaw=2 corpus pair (180° rotation). cv-local success_clean direct, recognizer output equals ground truth byte-for-byte (diffFromCorrected=0). Captured 2026-05-12 on cube-snap PR #99 Fixer flow. Adds coverage for the 'recover-from-one-borderline-grid' path: image B's L grid (gridId=12) has quality=12.6 with badSamples=1/suspectSamples=4.6 — well below the typical ~95 quality of other selected grids in this pair, but pipeline still produces the correct state.
Known cross-runner disagreement case; SHA fields are load-bearing and this local hashed pair is currently exact.

Added and calibrated 2026-05-25 from canonical /Users/jhuber/cube-corpus files to close the gcm_axis_ground_truth/corpus_manifest coverage gap for hull-label shadow/e2e validation. Category/score pinned from tools/probe_corpus.py on current recognizer: reject_retake, score 0/54.
Exact direct result; dynamic selected-face filtering ignores weak artifact grids.
Known partial face-orientation issue.

Known hard repaired/manual-review case. PR #72 orientation rerank raised this from 16/54 to 30/54; remaining wrong stickers are a follow-up diagnostic queue, and the selected repair's smallest orientation-rank diagnostic may differ from the pre-rerank candidate.
Exact direct case on the local hashed images. Promoted to success_clean after selected-grid quality calibration accepted marginal but supported direct unique legal states.

Known retake/reject case.

First yaw=2 corpus pair (180° rotation). cv-local success_clean direct path, recognizer output equals ground truth byte-for-byte (diffFromCorrected=0). Captured 2026-05-12 on cube-snap PR #99 Fixer flow; LLM provider claude-sonnet failed validation on this pair (10 white stickers) which is the expected LLM failure mode on non-canonical-yaw captures.

yaw=1 corpus pair. cv-local success_clean direct path, recognizer output equals ground truth byte-for-byte (diffFromCorrected=0). GT was originally saved by the Fixer in capture-frame yaw (centers UBRDFL) — yaw-normalized to canonical WCA via _capture_yaw_state_to_wca(state, 1) on 2026-05-14 before manifest wiring; cube-snap#105 prevents this case in the Fixer save path going forward. LLM provider gpt-5 failed validation on this pair (duplicated/missing centers, the expected LLM failure mode on non-canonical-yaw captures).
